title: Jungić et al. Conjecture on $T_k$
context_text: Jungić, Licht (Fox), Mahdian, Nešetřil, and Radoičić proved that there are positive constants $c, c'$ such that $c'k^2 \leq T_k \leq ck^3$, where $T_k$ is the smallest positive integer $t$ such that for every positive integer $m$, every equinumerous $t$-coloring of $[tm]$ contains a rainbow $k$-term arithmetic progression. They conjectured that the lower bound is correct, i.e., $T_k = \Theta(k^2)$, a problem reiterated in the survey of Jungić, Nešetřil, and Radoičić.
attributed_to: Jungić, Licht (Fox), Mahdian, Nešetřil, and Radoičić
paper_contribution: Proves $T_k \leq ck^2 \log k$ (Corollary 4), improving the previous upper bound of $ck^3$ and making progress toward the conjectured tight bound $T_k = \Theta(k^2)$.
